questions.question: What is the primary function of a D flip-flop in digital circuits?
options: Perform arithmetic operations.
Select one of many inputs to be routed to a single output.
Store a single bit of binary information.
Amplify an analog signal.
Generate clock signals.

questions.question: What is the main concept behind 'out-of-order execution' in modern CPUs?
options: To process instructions from multiple threads simultaneously.
To execute instructions in an order different from their program order, exploiting instruction-level parallelism.
To pre-fetch data from main memory before it is needed.
To reduce power consumption by selectively disabling idle functional units.
To ensure all instructions complete execution in program order.

questions.question: In the physical design flow of a digital IC, what is the primary goal of Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS)?
options: To reduce the overall power consumption of the chip.
To create a balanced clock distribution network to minimize clock skew and latency.
To place standard cells onto the silicon die.
To extract parasitic resistances and capacitances from the layout.
To verify the logical equivalence between RTL and gate-level netlists.

questions.question: In a virtual memory system, what is the primary role of the 'page table'?
options: To store the contents of recently accessed data blocks.
To map virtual addresses to physical addresses.
To manage the allocation of CPU registers.
To store instructions awaiting execution.
To control access to I/O devices.

questions.question: In semiconductor manufacturing, what does 'yield' primarily refer to?
options: The maximum operating frequency a chip can achieve.
The percentage of manufactured chips that meet all specified functional and performance requirements.
The total number of transistors integrated on a single die.
The power consumption of the chip during typical operation.
The time it takes to produce a batch of wafers.
